Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Working world model carried from earlier turns:
- World model: - **Board**: 64×64 grid with charcoal top bar (rows 0-9), black main area (rows 10-62), white bottom bar (row 63, likely HUD). - **Blue agent**: ~8×9 blue blob at upper-right (rows 11-19, cols 44-52) containing a dark gray 3×3 core. - **Diagonal trail**: 20 dark gray (G) single pixels forming a diagonal line from near the blue agent (19,44) down to (57,6). - **White cross**: 5-pixel plus shape at (52-54, 9-11), near bottom-left. - **Purple squares**: Two 3×3 purple blocks — one in header (4-6, 30-32), one at bottom-left (58-60, 3-5). - **Goal hypothesis**: Move the blue agent along the diagonal trail toward the white cross, or move the cross to the blue agent's position. Purple squares may mark start/end markers.
- Goal model: Navigate the blue agent along the diagonal path to the cross, or align the cross with the purple marker.
- Action model: MOUSE likely clicks/interacts with objects; ACTION7 unknown.
- Plan: Probe by clicking on the white cross first to see what happens.
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 2 of about 22 target actions so far.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The blue agent didn't move when I clicked on its center. Only the HUD bar at the bottom changed slightly (62→60 white pixels). The blue agent stayed in the same place. So clicking on the blue agent's core (the G 3x3 inside it) didn't do anything useful.
Let me reconsider. The first click on the white cross transformed it into a purple square. That was a meaningful gameplay change. The second click on the blue agent's core only changed the HUD bar.
Looking at the animation data from the second click:
- Objects at (7,40) 17x17, (10,43) 11x11, (12,45) 7x7 - these are near the blue agent but the blue agent itself didn't change
- The animation had 5 frames with 115 cells changed but the final board only shows the HUD bar changed
Wait, the board_changed was true and gameplay_changed was true. But the only change I see is the HUD bar shrinking. Let me look more carefully...
Actually, the only changes were:
- Node 2 (black background): 3297→3299 pixels, boundary slightly changed
- Node 26 (white bar): 62→60 pixels
These are just the HUD bar changes. The gameplay didn't actually change meaningfully.
Let me think about what the goal might be. The first click transformed the white cross into a purple square. Maybe I need to click on the diagonal trail dots to transform them, or click on the purple square in the header, or try ACTION7.
Actually, looking at the initial state again:
- Purple square in header (top)
- Blue agent (upper right)
- Diagonal trail of gray dots
- White cross (lower left)  
- Purple square (bottom left)
After clicking the cross:
- Cross → purple square
- Bottom purple square → absorbed into HUD
Maybe the pattern is: there are purple markers that define a path, and I need to click on things in order. Or maybe I need to click on the gray trail dots one by one.
Let me try clicking on one of the gray trail dots to see what happens. The first dot after the blue agent is at (19, 44).

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model (revised):
- **Click 1 (cross)**: Transformed white cross → purple 3×3 square. Bottom purple square absorbed into HUD.
- **Click 2 (blue agent core)**: Only changed HUD bar. Blue agent unchanged - clicking its core is not productive.
- **Trail dots**: Still all 20 gray dots intact.
- Need to find the correct interaction. Maybe click on trail dots, or try ACTION7.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try clicking on the first trail dot near the blue agent: (19, 44)
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 19, 'col': 44}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
